title: Chocolate Mousse
intro: Delia Smith taught Britain to cook, and her chocolate mousse is pure, dark and grown-up. The Thermomix melts the chocolate and whips the eggs, so it comes together in minutes.
steps: Place the chocolate in the bowl and chop 8 sec / speed 8. Melt 4 min / 50°C / speed 2, then tip into a bowl.
Insert the butterfly. Add the egg whites and salt and whip 3 min / speed 4 to firm peaks; set aside.
Without washing, add the yolks and sugar and mix 30 sec / speed 4. Add the melted chocolate and mix 10 sec / speed 3.
Fold the whipped whites through by hand, gently. Spoon into glasses and chill 2 hours
tips: Fold — don't stir — the whites in to keep it airy.
Use a good 70% dark chocolate for that grown-up edge.

faq.a: Whipped whites give the mousse its light, airy set without any cream.

faq.q: Is this the original Delia Smith recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Delia Smith and chocolate mousse; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Chocolate Mousse in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Delia Smith-style chocolate mousse: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
